ls.name: ls
ls.description: List files and directories in the current directory or in the paths you pass in.
options: -a  do not ignore entries starting with .
-l  use a long listing format
-h  with -l, print human readable sizes
-R  list subdirectories recursively

cd.name: cd
cd.description: Change the current working directory. With no argument, switch to the home directory.

pwd.name: pwd
pwd.summary: print name of current working directory
pwd.description: Print the absolute pathname of the current working directory.

mkdir.name: mkdir
options: -p  no error if existing, make parent directories as needed

nano.name: nano
nano.description: Open a fullscreen nano-style editor overlay for editing files in the virtual filesystem.

rm.name: rm
rm.description: Remove files and directories from the virtual file system.
options: -r, -R  remove directories and their contents recursively
-f  ignore nonexistent files and arguments, never prompt

cp.name: cp
cp.description: Copy files or directories to a new location.

mv.name: mv
mv.description: Move files or rename them inside the virtual file system.

touch.name: touch
touch.summary: change file timestamps or create files
touch.description: Create a file if it does not exist, or update its modification time.

cat.name: cat
cat.summary: concatenate files and print on the standard output
cat.description: Display file contents or pass stdin through unchanged.

head.name: head
head.summary: output the first part of files
head.description: Print the first lines from a file or from stdin.
options: -n NUM  print the first NUM lines

tail.name: tail
tail.summary: output the last part of files
tail.description: Print the last lines from a file or from stdin.
options: -n NUM  print the last NUM lines
-f  follow appended data (simulated)

less.name: less
less.description: View a file one screen at a time; in this terminal it is rendered as a static page ending marker.

grep.name: grep
grep.description: Search text for a pattern and print matching lines.
options: -i  ignore case distinctions
-n  print line numbers
-r  read all files under each directory, recursively
-v  invert the sense of matching
-c  print only a count of matching lines

find.name: find
find.summary: search for files in a directory hierarchy
find.description: Walk a directory tree and filter entries by name or type.
options: -name PATTERN  match file name with glob syntax
-type f|d  match files or directories

which.name: which
which.summary: shows the full path of shell commands
which.description: Locates a command by searching the PATH.

sort.name: sort
sort.description: Sort the input lines lexicographically or numerically.
options: -r  reverse the result of comparisons
-n  compare according to string numerical value
-u  output only the first of an equal run

uniq.name: uniq
uniq.description: Filter adjacent matching lines from sorted input.
options: -c  prefix lines by the number of occurrences
-d  only print duplicate lines

wc.name: wc
wc.summary: print newline, word, and byte counts
wc.description: Count lines, words, and characters in text.
options: -l  print line counts
-w  print word counts
-c  print byte counts

cut.name: cut
cut.summary: remove sections from each line of files
cut.description: Select specific fields from delimited text.

sed.name: sed
sed.summary: stream editor for filtering and transforming text
sed.description: Apply simple substitutions to each input line.

awk.name: awk
awk.description: Pattern-based text processing with field extraction support.

chmod.name: chmod
chmod.description: Change the permissions associated with a file or directory.

chown.name: chown
chown.description: Change the owner, and optionally the group, of a file or directory.

chgrp.name: chgrp
chgrp.description: Change the group ownership of files.

ps.name: ps
ps.summary: report a snapshot of current processes
ps.description: Show the simulated process table for the current session.

top.name: top
top.description: Show a live process snapshot with CPU and memory information.

kill.name: kill
kill.summary: send a signal to a process
kill.description: Send a signal to one or more simulated processes.
options: -9  forcefully terminate a process
-15  request graceful termination

tar.name: tar
tar.description: Create, inspect, or extract simulated tar archives.

zip.name: zip
zip.description: Create or modify a compressed archive.

gzip.name: gzip
gzip.description: Compress files using the gzip algorithm.

ping.name: ping
ping.summary: send ICMP ECHO_REQUEST packets to network hosts
ping.description: Test connectivity to a host with simulated ICMP replies.
options: -c N  stop after sending N packets

ifconfig.name: ifconfig
ifconfig.description: Display the simulated network interface configuration.

netstat.name: netstat
netstat.description: Show active sockets and listening ports in the simulation.

ssh.name: ssh
ssh.description: Attempt a simulated SSH connection to a remote host.

scp.name: scp
scp.description: Simulated secure copy client for remote transfer workflows.

apt.name: apt
apt.description: Manage the simulated package set used by the terminal.

df.name: df
df.summary: report file system disk space usage
df.description: Display available and used space for mounted filesystems.
options: -h  print sizes in human readable format

du.name: du
du.description: Summarize disk usage for files and directories.
options: -h  human readable sizes
-s  display only a total for each argument

free.name: free
free.summary: display amount of free and used memory in the system
free.description: Show memory usage for the simulated system.
options: -h  print human readable output

uname.name: uname
uname.description: Return information about the simulated kernel and host.

whoami.name: whoami
whoami.description: Display the name of the current user.

who.name: who
who.description: Show the current logged-in session information.

hostname.name: hostname
hostname.summary: show or set the system host name
hostname.description: Print the simulated host name for the terminal session.

id.name: id
id.summary: print real and effective user and group IDs
id.description: Display the current simulated UID, GID, and groups.

useradd.name: useradd
useradd.summary: create a new user or update default new user information
useradd.description: Create a new simulated user account and home directory.

userdel.name: userdel
userdel.summary: delete a user account and related files

passwd.name: passwd
passwd.description: Update the password for a simulated account.

echo.name: echo
echo.description: Print the given arguments to standard output with simple environment expansion.
options: -n  do not output the trailing newline

date.name: date
date.summary: print or set the system date and time
date.description: Show the current local date and time in the browser session.

cal.name: cal
cal.description: Render the current month in a compact terminal calendar.

history.name: history
history.description: Show commands entered in the current browser session.

clear.name: clear
clear.description: Clear the visible terminal output and reset the prompt line.

man.name: man
man.summary: an interface to the system reference manuals
man.description: Show detailed manual pages, summaries, or search results from the built-in static manual database.
options: -f, --whatis  display a one-line description for a manual page
-k, --apropos  search the one-line descriptions for a keyword

help.name: help
help.description: Show a categorized list of commands supported by the terminal.

env.name: env
env.description: Display the current shell environment variables.

exit.name: exit
exit.description: Exit the shell (simulated - cannot exit in browser).

sudo.name: sudo
sudo.summary: execute a command as another user
sudo.description: Execute a command with elevated privileges (simulated).
